stories.title: UN looks for a short-term solution for moving fertilizer through the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The U.N. looks for a quick solution to get cargo ships with fertilizer through the Strait of Hormuz, to prevent a global food crisis.

stories.title: How the conflict in Iran is testing the U.S.-European alliance
stories.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with correspondents Greg Myre and Rob Schmitz about how the Iran war is affecting the U.S. alliance with Europe.

stories.title: A complex set of negotiations to end Israel's overlapping wars
stories.description: Israel has agreed to a ceasefire in Lebanon, part of a complex web of Mideast negotiations, from Iran to Gaza.

stories.title: Iran War timeline continues to shift. And, jury rules Live Nation acted as monopoly
stories.description: Trump continues to try to declare victory in Iran as the timeline for the war's end shifts. And, a jury determined that Live Nation acted as a monopoly and overcharged ticket buyers.

stories.title: Israel starts a tense ceasefire in Lebanon, as Trump sounds optimistic on Iran talks
stories.description: The leaders of Israel and Lebanon agreed to a 10-day ceasefire in Israel's fight against Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon, but Israel vowed to keep troops in southern Lebanon.

stories.title: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.description: A 10-day truce between the two countries is now in effect, with Iran-backed Hezbollah voicing support, as negotiations continue between the US and Iran.

stories.title: Lyse Doucet: Under fragile ceasefire, Iranians wonder if US deal can be done
stories.description: The BBC's chief international correspondent reports from Iran as diplomatic efforts to avoid a return to war intensify.

stories.title: Israeli triple-tap strike kills three paramedics in Lebanon, officials say
stories.description: Lebanon's government condemns as a "flagrant crime" the killing of the paramedics, one of whom featured in a BBC report.

stories.title: Israel and Hezbollah continue attacks after Israel-Lebanon talks in US
stories.description: Israel strikes hit southern Lebanon and Hezbollah fires rockets at Israel, a day after rare direct negotiations.

stories.title: Sri Lanka repatriates 238 Iranian sailors stranded after US torpedo attack
stories.description: The survivors of the US attack on Iranian vessel Iris Dena, which claimed the lives of 104 people, were among those sent home.

stories.title: Trump hints Iran talks could resume this week as US port blockade continues
stories.description: The United Nations' secretary general said it was "highly probable" that the negotiations would restart.

stories.title: Lebanon seeks peace, but Hezbollah needs to be convinced first
stories.description: The Lebanese government go into peace talks with limited influence over the group.

stories.title: The Israeli town on the frontline with Hezbollah
stories.description: BBC foreign correspondent Nick Beake visits Metula, an Israeli town surrounded on three sides by Lebanon.

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon hold first direct talks since 1993
stories.description: A US statement said the two sides had agreed to launch direct negotiations, at a time and place to be determined.

stories.title: How many ships are crossing the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: Since the start of the US blockade on Monday, 15 vessels have crossed the Strait of Hormuz, nine of which have links to Iran, BBC Verify analysis of ship-tracking data suggests.

stories.title: Europe leads postwar security talks
stories.description: Donald Trump has said that another round of talks between the United States and Iran could take place as soon as this weekend. Meanwhile, this Friday, the French and British leaders are due to chair a meeting of around 40 countries focused on securing the Strait of Hormuz. France...

stories.title: Lebanon: Mass displacement after weeks of strikes
stories.description: There is a sense of both caution and hope. Although the Lebanese army had warned residents who fled the south not to return, footage shows vehicles already moving back toward the area. Six weeks of Israeli strikes displaced well over a million people—around one-fifth of Lebanon’s...

stories.title: Trump-Meloni relationship turn sour
stories.description: Far-right Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni was once considered one of Donald Trump’s closest allies in Europe, but their relationship has recently deteriorated. As Caitlin Brown reports, Meloni has fallen out of favor with the US president, in part due to her criticism of th...

stories.title: Europe faces looming jet fuel shortage: 'Six weeks of fuel left', energy boss warns
stories.description: Rising fuel costs have already prompted many airlines worldwide to raise ticket prices, passing the burden on to passengers. But as the crisis drags on and the Strait of Hormuz remains blocked, both Europe and Asia are now facing a severe fuel shortage that could lead to widespre...

stories.title: France, UK host talks on securing Hormuz
stories.description: French President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer are chairing a meeting of around 40 so-called “non-belligerent” countries this Friday to discuss securing the Strait of Hormuz. The closure of this vital route—now subject to both an Iranian blockade and a r...

stories.title: Lebanon army reports number of ceasefire violations
stories.description: A 10-day ceasefire agreement between Lebanon and Israel came into effect this Friday, though the Lebanese army has already reported several alleged violations in the southern border region, where these images were taken. US President Donald Trump announced the deal and said he ha...

stories.title: 'Allies, not vassals': How Meloni's break with Trump became a political moment for Italy
stories.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni is managing the repercussions of a public rebuke from US President Donald Trump this week over the pope, Iran and a defence deal with Israel. It's a rupture that had been building since the outbreak of the US-Israel war with Iran and may ulti...

stories.title: Macron, Starmer chair meeting to mull European post-war mission in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: French President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer will on Friday host a video conference with their European allies to discuss the possible deployment of a multinational force to the Strait of Hormuz once the war in Iran is over. The force would help ensure...

stories.title: Iran war’s big winners: Wall Street, weapons firms, AI and green energy
stories.description: The global economic outlook for 2026 looks grim if the Iran war continues, but some industries are booming.

stories.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 49 of the US-Iran conflict?
stories.description: Celebrations in Lebanon as a 10-day ceasefire begins, opening the door to talks on a longer-term Israel-Lebanon deal.

stories.title: Iran women football players thank Australian government for protection
stories.description: The two Iran footballers who stayed in Australia following the Asian Cup say they want to rebuild lives in 'safety'.

stories.title: ‘Vengeance for all’: How Iran’s Lego videos won narrative war against Trump
stories.description: The viral videos by Iranian creators, say analysts, are high-quality though cheap, and hit fissures in US politics.

stories.title: Why are anti-war protests in the West muted on Iran?
stories.description: Despite global opposition, Iran war protests lag behind Gaza and Ukraine, shaped by fatigue, fear and disillusionment.

stories.title: Trump calls war on Iran a ‘little diversion’
stories.description: US President Donald Trump has described the war on Iran which has killed more than 2,000 people as a ‘little diversion’.

stories.title: Celebrations in Lebanon as 10-day ceasefire with Israel begins
stories.description: Celebratory gunfire heard in Beirut as truce takes effect, though Lebanese military reports violations.

stories.title: How Israel’s war upended daily life in Lebanon
stories.description: People in Beirut describe how the Israel war has turned their lives upside down.

stories.title: Trump hints at Islamabad trip if Iran deal is finalised
stories.description: PRIME Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Qatar’s Emir Sheikh Tamim shake hands in Doha; and (right) Field Marshal Asim Munir meets Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf in Tehran.—Reuters/AFP • Claims Iran has accepted ‘almost everything’ in talks• Nuclear issue remains ke...

stories.title: PM Shehbaz to meet President Erdogan, attend diplomacy forum on third leg of tour today
stories.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif will meet Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan and attend the Antalya Diplomacy Forum today on the third leg of a tri-nation tour, the Prime Minister’s Office (PMO) said on Friday. In a statement on X, the PMO said that the prime minister will part...

stories.title: Russian strikes kill 19 across Ukraine
stories.description: KYIV: A massive Russian drone and missile barrage killed at least 19 people in cities across Ukraine, more than four years into the war with talks on ending the conflict stalled. Journalists heard loud bangs echoing over the capital during the night and saw huge plumes of black s...

stories.title: Israeli army fire kills Palestinian teenager in West Bank
stories.description: RAMALLAH: Israeli forces killed a 17-year-old Palestinian in the occupied West Bank on Thursday, according to Palestinian officials. Mohammed Murad Mahmud Rayan was “killed by the (Israeli) occupation forces on Thursday morning in Beit Duqqu”, a village just north of Jerusalem, t...

stories.title: ‘Handful of tyrants’ ravaging world peace, says pope
stories.description: BAMENDA/LONDON: Pope Leo XIV on Thursday criticised the “tyrants” ransacking the world, on a high-security visit to a “bloodstained” region of Cameroon following a war of words with US President Donald Trump. Since his landmark four-nation African tour kicked off earlier this wee...

stories.title: Iran war exposes cost of Asia’s fossil fuel reliance
stories.description: • Forces Bangladesh into costly spot market due to loss of LNG contracts• Solar expansion cuts Pakistan’s fuel import bill by billions over four years• Analysts urge faster global shift to clean power for reducing price shocks DHAKA: As a second major energy shock in four years r...

stories.title: Iran-Israel war LIVE: Hezbollah says 'finger on the trigger' if Israel violates truce
stories.description: A 10-day ceasefire agreed between Israel and Lebanon took effect on Thursday (April 16, 2026)

stories.title: Trump says it is important for Pope to understand Iran is a global threat
stories.description: "The Pope has to understand — it's ​very simple — Iran cannot ‌have a nuclear weapon. The world would be in great danger," says Mr. Trump

stories.title: Trump lambasts Pope Leo XIV, extending feud over Iran war with first American pontiff
stories.description: Pope Leo, who is known for choosing his words carefully, ‌has emerged as an outspoken ​critic of the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran that ⁠began on February 28

stories.title: Pakistan starts preparing for next round of U.S.-Iran talks: officials
stories.description: The U.S. and Iran held rare direct talks in Pakistan over the weekend aimed at ending their conflict, but the talks ended early Sunday (April 12, 2026) without any agreement

stories.title: Macron, Starmer hold international summit on reopening Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The U.S. is not part of the planning for what has been branded the Strait of Hormuz Maritime Freedom of Navigation Initiative

stories.title: Iran-Israel war on April 16, 2026: 10-day ceasefire deal between Israel, Lebanon takes effect midnight; Lebanon reports ceasefire violations
stories.description: Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah exchanged new cross-border fire shortly before a truce was due to take effect

stories.title: Press review: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire may fail and US reinstates oil sanctions on Russia
stories.description: Top stories from the Russian press on Friday, April 17th

stories.title: US aggression against Iran continues series of 'unipolar wars' — senior legislator
stories.description: Alexey Pushkov said that Iran did not pose any threat to the West

stories.title: Washington pressuring Europe to join conflict with Iran — newspaper
stories.description: El Mundo reported that significant tension surrounds the US-Israeli military operation in the Islamic Republic

stories.title: No legal basis for imposing fees in Strait of Hormuz — head of UN maritime agency
stories.description: According to Arsenio Dominguez, the world "only pays attention to maritime transport when it is affected, and this causes economic damage to people"
